food_subgroup: Fruit juice drinks / Packaged orange juice beverages

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301710 covers orange juice as a food product; Pokka Orange 1000 is a commercially packaged, sweetened orange juice drink — closest_parent used as no exact branded entry exists in FoodOn.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers orange juice generically; Pokka Orange 1000 is a branded, diluted/sweetened variant — closest_parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 129560003 covers orange juice as a substance; used as closest_parent since Pokka Orange 1000 is a branded packaged drink, not pure orange juice.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one 1000 ml bottle of Pokka Orange Juice Drink, commonly consumed as a single-serve bottled beverage in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, orange juice, canned — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 168998); cross-referenced with USDA Branded Food entry for Pokka orange juice drink
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Pokka Orange 1000 is a commercially packaged, lightly sweetened orange juice drink. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for canned orange juice (FDC ID 168998), which is the closest approved-source analogue. Sugar content is slightly elevated relative to pure orange juice to reflect added sugar in the commercial formulation. Sodium is low as typical for this product category. Values represent a reasonable per-100g estimate for this product type.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database entry for orange juice
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 50 is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for orange juice (unsweetened, commercially processed). Pokka Orange 1000 is a lightly sweetened orange juice drink; GI is estimated at 50 based on the closest analogous entry for commercial orange juice. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (50 × 104g) / 100 = 52.0, based on 1000g serving size and 10.4g carbohydrate per 100g.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (2 mg per 100g); moderate potassium (190 mg per 100g)
hypertension.guidance: Pokka Orange 1000 is very low in sodium and provides moderate potassium, making it generally acceptable for individuals with hypertension when consumed in moderate portions; however, the full 1000 ml bottle delivers a high total sugar load and should not be consumed in one sitting.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load per full serving (GL 52) and high free sugar content (9.8g per 100g / ~98g per bottle)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit Pokka Orange 1000 due to its high free sugar content and high glycemic load per bottle (GL ~52), which can cause rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation; if consumed, portion should be limited to no more than 150–200 ml.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free sugar content contributing to hypertriglyceridemia risk; negligible saturated fat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While Pokka Orange 1000 is very low in saturated fat and cholesterol, its high free sugar content (approximately 98g per bottle) poses a risk for elevated triglycerides and should be consumed sparingly by individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pokka Orange 1000 is a commercially packaged, pasteurized orange juice drink produced by Pokka Corporation and widely sold in Singapore. It is available in a 1000 ml bottle and is made from water, orange juice concentrate, sugar, citric acid, and added vitamin C. The drink has a sweet, tangy orange flavour with a smooth, liquid texture typical of commercially processed juice drinks. It is not a 100% pure orange juice product; it contains added sugar and flavourings, classifying it as an ultra-processed beverage. Per 100g, it provides approximately 44 kcal, 10.4g carbohydrate (of which 9.8g is sugar), 0.5g protein, and 0.1g fat. It is a notable source of vitamin C (approximately 50 mg per 100g due to fortification) and provides moderate potassium (190 mg per 100g). The glycemic index is estimated at 50 (low GI category) based on University of Sydney GI Database data for commercial orange juice; however, the glycemic load per full 1000 ml bottle is high (GL ~52), making it clinically significant for individuals managing blood glucose. Clinically, this beverage is of high concern for Type 2 Diabetes due to its high free sugar and glycemic load, moderate concern for cardiovascular disease due to hypertriglyceridemia risk from free sugars, and low concern for hypertension due to its very low sodium content. It is suitable for vegetarians, vegans, and halal diets. Commonly served as a table beverage in Singaporean noodle restaurants and hawker-adjacent food establishments.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Pokka Orange Juice?
Is Pokka Orange drink good for diabetics?
Pokka orange juice GI glycemic index
Is Pokka Orange 1000 suitable for hypertension?
How much sugar in a bottle of Pokka Orange?

confidence_reason: Pokka Orange 1000 is a branded commercial product sold in Singapore. No exact entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods for this specific product. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy canned orange juice (FDC ID 168998), which is the closest approved-source analogue. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database for commercial orange juice. Confidence is medium due to the use of an adapted rather than exact source match.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 168998, canned orange juice) as no exact branded entry for Pokka Orange 1000 was found in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods at time of processing. Sugar content is slightly elevated relative to pure orange juice to reflect the commercial formulation with added sugar. 2. protein_g (0.5g) and fat_g (0.1g) are present at trace levels consistent with diluted orange juice drink formulations; these are not zero. saturated_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy source for this product category; naturally zero by product formulation. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are 0.0 — naturally zero by product formulation for this beverage type. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA source. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero as this is a plant-derived juice drink. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero as this is a plant-derived beverage with no B12 fortification reported. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero; not reported in source and not fortified in this product. 3. GI of 50 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for commercial orange juice; gi_category is low (GI ≤ 55). Glycemic load per serving is calculated for the full 1000 ml bottle: GL = (50 × 104g carbohydrate) / 100 = 52.0. 4. Ontology codes use closest_parent match type as no exact branded entry exists in FoodOn, FoodEx2, or SNOMED CT for Pokka Orange 1000. 5. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as this product is sold and consumed in Singapore. 6. monounsaturated_fat_g set to 0.0 (naturally zero by product formulation for this diluted juice drink). polyunsaturated_fat_g set to 0.0 (naturally zero by product formulation). All -1 sentinel values in the per_100g block are not present; all fields have been populated with numeric values from the adapted USDA source or set to 0.0 where confirmed zero by formulation.
